pattern: A single left-to-right scan comparing each digit only to its immediate neighbor is enough, since the rule never looks further than one position away.
hints: You only ever need to compare a digit to the one right after it, never to any digit further away.
Walk the string once, converting each pair of neighboring characters to their numeric digit values before comparing.
The moment any neighboring pair differs by more than 2, you can stop and answer false immediately; only if every pair passes do you answer true.

edge_inputs.description: repeated equal digits, zero difference throughout
a descending run where every gap is exactly 2
the shortest possible string, at the minimum length
the longest possible string, all one repeated digit
a gap of exactly 2, the boundary of what still counts as acceptable
a gap of exactly 3, just past the acceptable boundary
the largest possible single-step gap, digits 9 and 0 adjacent

stress.description: random digit strings across the full allowed length range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 100))
    s = ''.join(str(rng.randint(0, 9)) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
